messages.content:
Company: T-Mobile
Department: Network Operations & Engineering
Industry: Telecommunications - Mobile Network Operator
Use Case Category: Infrastructure Monitoring & Network Observability
Primary Interest: Infrastructure Monitoring, Logs, Custom Metrics, Anomaly Detection
Scale & Environment:

50,000+ cell sites (eNodeBs, gNodeBs) across nationwide 4G LTE and 5G NR networks
100M+ active subscribers generating billions of signaling events daily
Multi-vendor RAN environment (Ericsson, Nokia, Samsung)
Core network infrastructure: MME, SGW, PGW, HSS clusters across multiple regions
15+ regional Network Operations Center (NOC) teams operating 24/7
Integration with OSS/BSS platforms (Amdocs, Ericsson OSS, ServiceNow)


Pain Points
Network Visibility & Observability Challenges
Fragmented subscriber experience visibility across network domains: T-Mobile's network monitoring is divided across vendor-specific Element Management Systems (Ericsson ENM, Nokia NetAct), OSS platforms for fault management, and separate RAN analytics tools. When a subscriber experiences poor call quality or dropped connections, NOC engineers must manually correlate data from multiple systems to identify whether the issue originates in the radio layer (RSRP/RSRQ degradation, handover failures), core network (MME congestion, S1 interface issues), or transport network (backhaul latency). This fragmentation adds 15-30 minutes to mean time to detection (MTTD) for subscriber-impacting incidents and prevents holistic analysis of end-to-end service quality.
Reactive troubleshooting driven by subscriber complaints rather than proactive detection: The current monitoring approach relies heavily on threshold-based alarms from individual network elements and escalated trouble tickets from customer care. NOC teams lack the ability to detect subtle performance degradation patterns—such as gradual increases in attachment failure rates at specific cell sites or growing RRC connection establishment latency—before they accumulate into widespread service impact. This reactive posture results in an average MTTR of 2-4 hours for cell site issues and significant customer churn risk due to undetected chronic performance problems.
Inability to correlate network events with subscriber behavior patterns: T-Mobile generates massive volumes of S1-MME signaling logs, X2 handover records, and radio measurements, but lacks an integrated platform to perform temporal correlation analysis. When investigating incidents like sudden spikes in data session failures or abnormal cell reselection patterns, engineers cannot easily identify common factors such as recent software pushes to specific MME pools, localized weather events affecting specific cell sectors, or unusual traffic patterns (conferences, sporting events). Critical questions like "Which MME hosts are experiencing authentication failures correlating with specific cell sites?" require hours of manual log analysis across disparate systems.
Data Platform & Analytics Limitations
Insufficient real-time analytics for high-cardinality network data: Core network signaling generates extraordinarily high-cardinality datasets with millions of unique IMSIs, tens of thousands of cell IDs (ECGI/NCGI), and hundreds of procedure types. Existing solutions using traditional relational databases or basic log aggregation tools struggle to perform real-time cardinality analysis needed for anomaly detection—such as identifying cell sites with abnormal subscriber churn rates, tracking unique IMSI patterns indicating device issues, or detecting coordinated failures across multiple eNodeBs. Current batch processing approaches introduce 15-60 minute delays in detecting emerging problems, during which thousands of subscribers may be affected.
Lack of standardized KPIs and metrics framework across NOC teams: Regional NOC teams have developed inconsistent approaches to measuring network health, with different definitions for key metrics like "call drop rate," varying thresholds for acceptable handover success rates (ranging from 95% to 98.5% across regions), and incompatible dashboards for similar use cases. This inconsistency prevents enterprise-wide benchmarking, makes it difficult to identify best practices from high-performing regions, and complicates executive reporting on nationwide network quality. Leadership lacks a single source of truth for critical metrics like subscriber experience scores or cell site availability trends.
Operational Efficiency & Collaboration
Extended mean time to repair due to manual root cause analysis workflows: When a cell site degradation is detected, NOC engineers follow time-consuming manual processes: extracting logs from the affected eNodeB via vendor EMS, pulling related MME signaling records, checking for recent RF optimization changes in planning systems, reviewing weather conditions, and manually correlating subscriber complaint patterns from CRM systems. This investigative work, performed across multiple logins and interfaces, extends MTTR by 30-90 minutes per incident. For high-priority sites affecting large subscriber populations or enterprise customers, these delays translate directly into revenue impact and SLA violations.

Key Metrics & Data Sources
Core Network Signaling Metrics (via Filebeat + Custom Log Parsing)
Collection Method: Filebeat deployed on MME, SGW, PGW nodes with custom parsing pipeline in Logstash to extract structured fields from S1-MME protocol logs and GTP-C messages. Alternative: Direct streaming from network probes (e.g., NETSCOUT InfiniStream) to Kafka → Logstash → Elasticsearch.

Procedure Success/Failure Rates:

Field names: network.procedure.id, network.procedure.result_code, network.procedure.result_message
Description: Success and failure counts for critical procedures including Initial Attach, Service Request, TAU, Handover, PDN Connection Establishment
Source: MME S1-MME logs, parsed from vendor-specific formats (Ericsson Event Files, Nokia S1-AP logs)
Breakdown: By host (MME hostname), network.provider (TMO/TMUS), network.procedure.type, event.outcome (success/failure)


Unique Subscriber Activity:

Field names: network.imsi, network.imei, network.msisdn (hashed for privacy)
Description: Cardinality tracking of unique subscribers per cell site, tracking pool, or MME to identify concentration anomalies
Source: Extracted from S1-MME signaling messages (Attach Request, Service Request, TAU)
Analysis: Enables spike detection for abnormal subscriber counts indicating network issues or device problems


Mobility & Handover Metrics:

Field names: network.handover.type, network.handover.source_cell_id, network.handover.target_cell_id, network.handover.outcome
Description: X2 and S1 handover success rates, handover preparation/execution times
Source: MME handover completion messages, eNodeB X2 signaling logs
Breakdown: By cell neighbor pair, tracking area, handover type (intra-freq, inter-freq, inter-RAT)


Authentication & Authorization Events:

Field names: network.auth.result, network.auth.failure_cause, network.subscriber.apn, network.subscriber.profile_type
Description: HSS authentication responses, APN authorization results, subscriber profile validation
Source: S6a interface logs between MME and HSS, APN-AMBR enforcement logs
Critical for: Detecting HSS synchronization issues, subscription provisioning errors, roaming problems



Radio Access Network (RAN) Metrics (via Filebeat + API Integration)
Collection Method: Filebeat collecting Performance Management (PM) counters from vendor EMS systems (Ericsson ENM, Nokia NetAct), supplemented by streaming API integrations for real-time metrics. Alternative: Direct polling of vendor APIs via Metricbeat HTTP module.

Cell Site Availability & Utilization:

Field names: network.cell.id (ECGI/NCGI format), network.cell.availability_pct, network.cell.prb_utilization_dl_pct, network.cell.prb_utilization_ul_pct
Description: Cell availability percentage, Physical Resource Block (PRB) utilization for downlink/uplink, active RRC connections
Source: eNodeB/gNodeB PM counters collected via Ericsson BSIM, Nokia NetAct PM
Update frequency: 15-minute granularity for PM files, 1-minute for streaming metrics


Radio Signal Quality:

Field names: network.radio.rsrp_dbm, network.radio.rsrq_db, network.radio.sinr_db, network.radio.cqi_avg
Description: Reference Signal Received Power, Reference Signal Received Quality, Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio, Channel Quality Indicator
Source: Measurement reports from UEs collected by eNodeB, aggregated in vendor PM systems
Analysis: Statistical distributions (P50, P95, P99) to identify poorly performing sectors


RRC Connection & Accessibility Metrics:

Field names: network.rrc.connection_attempts, network.rrc.connection_success, network.rrc.connection_failure_cause, network.rrc.setup_time_ms
Description: RRC connection establishment success rates, failure causes (congestion, capacity, timer expiry)
Source: eNodeB L3 counters from PM files
Critical thresholds: <98% success rate indicates cell issues


Call & Data Session Establishment:

Field names: network.voice.call_setup_success_rate, network.voice.call_drop_rate, network.data.session_setup_success_rate, network.data.session_drop_rate
Description: Voice call setup success (VoLTE CSFB), call drop rates, EPS bearer establishment success
Source: Aggregated from both RAN PM and core network signaling
Business impact: Directly correlates to subscriber experience and churn risk



Cell Site Infrastructure Metrics (via Metricbeat + Custom Modules)
Collection Method: Metricbeat System module on cell site routers/baseband units, custom module for vendor-specific SNMP/NetConf interfaces.

Backhaul Transport Performance:

Field names: network.transport.latency_ms, network.transport.jitter_ms, network.transport.packet_loss_pct, network.interface.bandwidth_utilization_pct
Description: Round-trip latency on S1/X2 interfaces, jitter affecting VoLTE quality, packet loss rates, interface utilization
Source: Cell site routers (Cisco, Juniper) via SNMP or streaming telemetry
Critical for: Identifying transport network bottlenecks causing signaling delays or call quality issues


Cell Site Equipment Health:

Field names: host.name, system.cpu.total.pct, system.memory.used.pct, host.uptime, hardware.temperature.celsius, hardware.alarm.status
Description: CPU/memory utilization on baseband units, equipment temperature, hardware alarms
Source: Metricbeat System module, vendor SNMP MIBs for temperature/alarm monitoring
Predictive maintenance: High CPU or temperature trends indicate impending failures



Subscriber Experience & Behavior Metrics (via APM Agents + Custom Instrumentation)
Collection Method: Custom metrics from subscriber analytics platforms, mobile app telemetry (RUM agent in T-Mobile app), and synthesized data from network probing.

End-to-End Service Quality Indicators:

Field names: subscriber.experience.score, subscriber.service.throughput_mbps_dl, subscriber.service.throughput_mbps_ul, subscriber.service.video_stall_rate
Description: Calculated subscriber experience scores (0-100), actual achieved throughput, video streaming quality metrics
Source: DPI platforms (Procera, Sandvine), mobile app instrumentation via Elastic APM RUM
Calculation: Composite score from latency, throughput, retransmission rates, application-specific QoE


Subscriber Complaint & Trouble Ticket Correlation:

Field names: ticket.id, ticket.category, ticket.symptom, ticket.affected_service, ticket.associated_cell_id, ticket.imsi (anonymized)
Description: Structured trouble ticket data from ServiceNow ITSM, correlated with network events
Source: ServiceNow API integration via HTTP JSON input in Logstash
Value: Enables correlation of subscriber-reported issues with specific network KPIs and cell sites



Anomaly Detection & Derived Metrics (via Elasticsearch Aggregations + ML)
Collection Method: Calculated within Elasticsearch using aggregation pipelines, transforms, and ML anomaly detection jobs.

Change Rate Detection for Signaling Anomalies:

Field names: network.anomaly.imsi_churn_rate_pct, network.anomaly.cell_id_volatility, network.anomaly.procedure_failure_spike
Description: Derived metrics showing percentage change in unique IMSI counts per time interval, cell ID volatility, spike detection for procedure failures
Calculation: Serial difference aggregations with lag windows, percentage change calculations via bucket_script
Alert thresholds: >400 IMSI count change or >50 cell ID count change in 5-minute window indicates cell site issues


Predictive Indicators via ML:

Field names: ml.anomaly_score, ml.typical_value, ml.actual_value, ml.multi_bucket_impact
Description: ML-detected anomalies in time-series metrics (call drop rates, handover failures, RRC setup times)
Source: Elasticsearch Machine Learning jobs running on time-series data
Models: Population analysis for comparing cell sites, individual analysis for single-cell baseline deviations




Use Cases
1. Real-Time Cell Site Performance Degradation Detection
Objective: Proactively identify cell sites experiencing performance degradation before subscriber impact escalates, reducing MTTD from 30+ minutes to under 5 minutes.
Implementation:

Deploy Filebeat on all MME nodes to stream S1-MME signaling logs (Attach, Service Request, TAU procedures) to Elasticsearch via Logstash parsing pipeline
Configure real-time aggregation pipelines using Elasticsearch Transform to calculate rolling 5-minute and 15-minute windows for key cell-level KPIs: procedure success rates, unique IMSI cardinality, handover success rates
Implement serial difference aggregations with configurable lag periods to detect sudden changes in subscriber counts per cell site (indicating cell degradation causing users to camp on alternate cells)
Use bucket_script aggregations to calculate percentage changes and flag spikes: >400 unique IMSI change = major cell issue, >50 cell neighbor change = handover problem
Create multi-threshold alerting: Yellow alert at 20% degradation in success rates, Red alert at 40% degradation or sustained anomalies over multiple intervals
Configure bucket_selector to filter out partial data buckets (minimum 200 events per interval to ensure statistical significance)

Key Metrics:

network.cell.id cardinality per 5-minute window with serial_diff lag analysis
network.procedure.success_rate_pct by procedure type and cell ID
network.anomaly.imsi_churn_rate_pct calculated as percentage change in unique IMSI counts
network.anomaly.cell_id_volatility measuring handover pattern changes
network.procedure.result_code distribution for root cause categorization (17=auth failure, 22=congestion, 19=network failure)

Output/Benefits:

Automated detection of cell site issues with specific failure categorization: "Cell 310260-1-9560842 experiencing 64% spike in 'UE not authorized' failures + 43% IMSI churn - likely HLR/HSS sync issue"
Proactive alerting enables NOC teams to dispatch field technicians or initiate remote troubleshooting before complaint volume escalates
Reduced MTTD from 30-45 minutes (complaint-driven) to 3-5 minutes (anomaly-detected)
Dashboard showing real-time heatmap of cell site health scores across all 50,000+ sites, color-coded by severity

2. Multi-Dimensional Network Incident Root Cause Analysis
Objective: Enable rapid root cause identification for subscriber-impacting incidents by correlating cell site performance, core network events, transport metrics, and external factors in a unified view.
Implementation:

Centralize logs from RAN (eNodeB PM files via Filebeat), Core (MME/SGW signaling via Filebeat), Transport (cell site router NetFlow/sFlow via Logstash), and OSS systems (configuration changes, software versions via API polling)
Enrich events at ingestion with contextual metadata: cell site geographic coordinates, serving MME pool, backhaul circuit IDs, recent configuration change timestamps from network planning systems
Create Kibana Canvas workbook template for incident investigation with pre-configured panels: timeline of key events, geographic scatter plot of affected cells, top affected subscribers (anonymized), failure code distribution, correlated alarms from fault management systems
Implement Elastic observability correlation UI to link related events across indices: e.g., spike in "22 Congestion" errors on MME-SF-01 correlating temporally with 85%+ PRB utilization on 15 nearby cell sites
Configure ML anomaly detection jobs to establish baselines for normal behavior by cell site, time of day, and day of week, flagging deviations
Build drilldown navigation: NOC dashboard → Regional anomaly summary → Specific cell site → Individual subscriber sessions → Detailed signaling message logs

Key Metrics:

network.procedure.result_code distribution over incident time window
network.cell.prb_utilization_dl_pct and network.transport.latency_ms correlation
network.cell.availability_pct trending before/during/after incident
config.change.timestamp from OSS showing recent RF parameter modifications
ml.anomaly_score highlighting which metrics deviated most from normal baselines
ticket.count aggregated by symptom and cell site for validation against network data

Output/Benefits:

Investigation time reduced from 60-90 minutes of manual log correlation to 10-15 minutes using guided Canvas workbooks
Example root cause insight: "43 cell sites in Denver metro showing handover failures due to X2 interface latency spike (125ms → 340ms) on Comcast backhaul circuits following 3:42pm route change"
Clear handoff documentation for escalation: single Canvas export PDF containing timeline, affected subscribers, correlated metrics, and recommended remediation steps
Post-incident review capability: Archive incident workspace for knowledge base and training new NOC engineers

3. Subscriber-Centric Experience Monitoring & Troubleshooting
Objective: Provide NOC and customer care teams with complete visibility into individual subscriber network experience, enabling rapid resolution of customer complaints with network-validated evidence.
Implementation:

Create unified subscriber index aggregating all network touches: IMSI-based correlation of core network signaling events, cell site attachment history, active sessions, device type/capabilities from HSS
Implement real-time session tracking: when subscriber initiates data session or voice call, track complete lifecycle with transaction tracing: Initial Attach → Authentication → Bearer Establishment → Handovers → Session Termination
Use APM transaction correlation to link mobile app performance (captured via RUM agent in T-Mobile app) with underlying network metrics: app slow video buffering correlated with cell site congestion or transport latency
Build subscriber detail dashboard accessible from ServiceNow integration: customer care agent enters phone number, dashboard displays last 24 hours of network events, cell sites visited, service success rates, quality metrics (RSRP/RSRQ), any recent network outages in subscriber's area
Configure behavioral anomaly detection: flag subscribers exhibiting unusual patterns (frequent authentication failures, excessive cell reselections, consistently poor radio conditions) indicating device malfunction or SIM issues
Implement privacy controls: full IMSI/MSISDN display restricted to authorized personnel, anonymized views for broader NOC team, automatic PII redaction in exported reports

Key Metrics:

network.imsi and network.msisdn (access-controlled) as correlation keys
subscriber.experience.score calculated from composite network performance indicators
network.cell.id visit history showing mobility pattern and camp-on durations
network.radio.rsrp_dbm and network.radio.sinr_db trends during subscriber's sessions
network.procedure.result_code filtered by subscriber, showing any failure patterns
transaction.duration.us from APM RUM agent correlated with network session metrics

Output/Benefits:

Customer care resolution time improvement: "Subscriber reports slow data speeds in Oakland - NOC dashboard shows customer camped on congested cell 310260-1-1730211 (94% PRB utilization) for 47 minutes, recommend suggest WiFi calling or drive-test cell for capacity expansion"
Proactive subscriber outreach: identify high-value enterprise customers affected by network incidents before they call, enabling proactive service credits
Device problem identification: "Subscriber IMSI 310260-XXXXX shows 47 authentication failures across 12 different cells over 3 days - likely SIM card issue, recommend SIM replacement"
QoS validation for enterprise SLAs: demonstrate network performance metrics to business customers with guaranteed service levels

4. Intelligent Anomaly Detection for Emerging Network Patterns
Objective: Leverage machine learning to detect subtle performance degradation trends, capacity saturation patterns, and unusual network behavior that threshold-based alarms miss, enabling predictive interventions.
Implementation:

Configure Elastic ML anomaly detection jobs for multiple analysis types:

Population analysis: Compare each cell site's call drop rate against population of similar cells (same vendor, same radio band, similar subscriber density) to identify outliers
Time series analysis: Establish baseline behavior patterns for metrics like RRC connection setup time per cell, flagging gradual degradation trends before hard thresholds breach
Multi-metric analysis: Detect unusual combinations of metrics (e.g., normal call volume but elevated RSRQ variance suggesting RF interference)


Deploy ML jobs across key metric categories: procedure success rates, handover performance, radio quality indicators, transport latency, equipment CPU utilization
Implement influencer analysis to identify contributing factors: when ML flags cell performance anomaly, automatically surface correlated dimensions like time of day, nearby cells also affected, recent software version changes
Create tiered alerting based on anomaly severity: Low severity (score 50-74) generates Slack notifications to on-call engineer, Medium (75-90) creates ServiceNow incident, Critical (>90) triggers escalation to senior NOC supervisor
Use ML forecasting to predict capacity exhaustion: identify cells trending toward PRB saturation, estimate days until intervention required
Configure seasonal learning to distinguish expected patterns (traffic surges during commute hours, sporting events, concerts) from true anomalies

Key Metrics:

ml.anomaly_score with thresholds: 0-49 (normal), 50-74 (low), 75-90 (medium), >90 (critical)
ml.typical_value vs ml.actual_value showing deviation magnitude
ml.bucket_influencers identifying which dimensions (cell ID, procedure type, time of day) contribute to anomaly
ml.multi_bucket_impact measuring sustained vs transient anomalies
All underlying network KPIs feeding ML jobs: success rates, latencies, counts, quality indicators

Output/Benefits:

Predictive detection: "Cell 310260-1-6697998 shows gradual RRC setup time increase from 250ms baseline to 410ms over 72 hours (ML anomaly score: 82) - recommend scheduled RF optimization before hard threshold breach"
Capacity planning insights: "ML forecasting indicates 47 cell sites in Chicago metro will reach >95% PRB utilization within 45 days based on current traffic growth trends - prioritize capacity expansion projects"
Pattern recognition across fleet: "ML population analysis identifies 127 cells running Ericsson L19A software version with 3.2% higher call drop rates than population average - potential software defect, recommend vendor investigation"
False positive reduction: ML baselines reduce noise from expected traffic surges, letting NOC focus on genuine problems

5. End-to-End Network Performance Benchmarking & Optimization
Objective: Establish data-driven network optimization workflows by enabling objective performance comparison across cell sites, vendors, regions, and technologies (LTE vs 5G NR).
Implementation:

Create standardized KPI dashboards using Kibana Lens with consistent metric definitions across all regions: Call Drop Rate (events where RRC Release-Indicator=User-Inactivity excluded), Handover Success Rate (X2+S1 combined), Average UE Throughput (DPI-measured, not theoretical maximum)
Implement cohort analysis using Elasticsearch transforms: group cell sites by relevant attributes (vendor equipment, RF band, urban/suburban/rural classification, backhaul type) for like-to-like comparisons
Build executive scorecards showing top/bottom performing regions, markets, and cell clusters with drill-down capability to individual sites
Configure automated test case execution: synthetic traffic generation (using network test equipment) with results fed to Elasticsearch, enabling controlled performance validation after network changes
Develop optimization workflow: identify underperforming cell clusters via ML population analysis → Run diagnostic report generating specific parameter recommendations → Track improvement after parameter adjustment → Document successful optimizations in searchable knowledge base
Create competitive benchmarking views: compare T-Mobile network KPIs against industry benchmarks (Opensignal, Ookla Speedtest data) using enriched context

Key Metrics:

Standardized KPI definitions stored as Elasticsearch runtime fields for consistency:

kpi.call_drop_rate = (Call Drops / Call Attempts) * 100, excluding normal releases
kpi.handover_success_rate = (Successful Handovers / Handover Attempts) * 100
kpi.rrc_success_rate = (RRC Setup Complete / RRC Setup Attempts) * 100


network.cell.vendor, network.cell.band, network.cell.classification for cohort grouping
performance.test.throughput_mbps, performance.test.latency_ms from synthetic testing
optimization.parameter.{name} tracking before/after values for RF parameters (PCI, antenna tilt, transmit power)

Output/Benefits:

Data-driven optimization: "Cells using Ericsson RBS 6000 series with 2-degree downtilt achieve 12% better handover success rates than 4-degree configuration - recommend parameter sweep across remaining 340 sites"
Regional best practice sharing: "Seattle NOC's aggressive PCI planning strategy (6-cell reuse pattern vs 3-cell) shows 8% lower interference - replicate approach in Phoenix market"
Executive visibility: "Nationwide LTE call drop rate improved from 0.89% to 0.67% over Q3 after targeted optimization of worst-performing 5% of cells, validated by Elasticsearch tracking"
Vendor accountability: Objective performance comparison enables negotiation leverage: "Nokia equipment showing 15% higher RRC failure rates than Ericsson baseline in comparable suburban deployments"

6. Proactive Network Capacity Planning & Congestion Management
Objective: Use historical trending and predictive analytics to identify cells approaching capacity limits, optimize traffic distribution, and plan infrastructure investments with data-driven justification.
Implementation:

Configure Elasticsearch data tiers with Index Lifecycle Management: Hot tier (SSD, 7 days) for real-time monitoring, Warm tier (SSD/HDD mix, 90 days) for trend analysis, Cold tier (HDD, 18 months) for capacity planning historical data
Use Elasticsearch rollup/downsampling to aggregate high-resolution metrics (1-minute) into hourly/daily summaries for long-term trending without excessive storage costs
Implement transform jobs calculating busy-hour statistics: identify peak traffic period per cell site, calculate 95th percentile PRB utilization during busy hour
Build capacity heatmaps: geographic visualization showing cell utilization percentiles, color-coded by urgency (green <60%, yellow 60-80%, orange 80-95%, red >95%)
Create "what-if" scenario modeling: simulate impact of adding new sectors, changing antenna azimuths, or deploying small cells using historical traffic patterns
Configure alerting for sustained high utilization: Yellow alert when cell exceeds 75% PRB utilization for >2 hours during busy period for 3+ consecutive days (trending toward capacity exhaustion)
Integrate with network planning tools: automated export of congested cell list to RF planning software (Atoll, Asset) for optimization studies

Key Metrics:

network.cell.prb_utilization_dl_pct and network.cell.prb_utilization_ul_pct with statistical aggregations (p50, p95, p99)
network.cell.active_users_avg during busy hour
network.cell.throughput_mbps actual vs theoretical maximum
capacity.busy_hour.start_time and capacity.busy_hour.prb_utilization_p95 from transform job
capacity.growth_rate_pct_monthly calculated from historical trends using moving averages
capacity.estimated_days_to_saturation from ML forecasting

Output/Benefits:

Prioritized investment planning: "234 cell sites forecast to exceed capacity within 90 days based on 12-month trend analysis - estimated $4.2M investment required for sector splits/upgrades, prioritized list ranked by subscriber impact"
Traffic steering optimization: "Cell 310260-1-3697302 consistently congested during evening commute (PRB >92%) while neighbor cells at 54% - recommend adjusted handover parameters to load-balance traffic"
Financial justification for infrastructure: "Executive summary showing $15M capex request for 5G densification in Manhattan will address 89 congested sites affecting 47,000 subscribers daily, projected to reduce complaints by 35% and churn risk by 2.1%"
Seasonal planning: "Historical data shows 18% traffic surge during Q4 holiday shopping period - proactively augment capacity at 67 mall/retail corridor sites before Black Friday"

7. Network Operations Command Center Unified Dashboard
Objective: Provide executive and NOC leadership with real-time, comprehensive situational awareness of nationwide network health, ongoing incidents, and operational KPIs in a unified command center view.
Implementation:

Design Kibana Canvas "Network NOC Command Center" template with key sections:

Top Banner: At-a-glance current status - nationwide call success rate, active P1/P2 incidents, subscriber experience score, total cells in alarm state
Geographic View: US map with color-coded cell site availability by market, clickable regions drilling into local issues
Incident Timeline: Horizontal timeline showing current incidents with time-to-detect, assigned owner, status (investigating/mitigating/resolved)
Top Offenders: Lists of worst-performing cells, MME hosts, transport circuits with change indicators (improving/degrading)
Trend Charts: Week-over-week comparison of key KPIs, highlighting improvements or regressions
Alert Stream: Real-time scrolling feed of ML anomalies and threshold alerts, filterable by severity/region


Implement auto-refresh at 60-second intervals for real-time updates
Configure role-based access: Executive view (high-level KPIs only), NOC Supervisor view (full detail + incident management controls), Regional NOC view (filtered to specific geography)
Integrate ServiceNow ITSM: Display active incidents with ownership, SLA countdown timers, escalation status
Build mobile-responsive version: enable NOC managers to monitor status from tablets/phones during emergency response
Create automated executive reporting: scheduled Canvas PDF exports emailed to leadership weekly with network performance summary

Key Metrics:

Nationwide aggregate KPIs:

kpi.network_availability_pct = (Available Cell-Minutes / Total Cell-Minutes) * 100
kpi.call_success_rate_pct = successful calls / total call attempts
kpi.subscriber_experience_score_avg = weighted average across all active sessions


incident.count by priority (P1/P2/P3) and status (new/investigating/mitigating/resolved)
incident.mttr_minutes_avg and incident.mttd_minutes_avg with trend indicators
network.cell.alarm_count by severity and category (hardware/software/performance)
Regional breakdown: top 5 markets by subscriber count showing local KPIs vs national benchmark

Output/Benefits:

Executive confidence: Single pane of glass for C-suite to monitor network health during board meetings or crisis situations: "CTO can answer 'How's the network?' question with specific, real-time data rather than anecdotal reports"
Rapid incident triage: NOC supervisors can quickly identify multiple incidents with common root cause (e.g., 15 separate cell alarms all linked to single router failure in regional hub)
Performance accountability: Weekly automated reports show which regional NOC teams are meeting SLAs vs underperforming, driving operational improvements
Historical benchmarking: "Q4 2025 showed 22% reduction in P1 incidents compared to Q4 2024, and 31% improvement in MTTR - quantifiable evidence of observability investment ROI"

8. Automated Network Event Correlation & Root Cause Inference
Objective: Reduce time spent on manual log correlation and root cause analysis by automatically linking related events across network domains and suggesting probable causes based on historical incident patterns.
Implementation:

Configure Elastic observability APM correlation features to link related events across multiple indices (RAN logs, Core logs, Transport logs, OSS change records) within time windows
Implement event enrichment pipeline: as MME procedure failures are ingested, automatically query cell site status from RAN index, recent configuration changes from OSS index, transport latency from router index, and append as nested fields
Create "incident signature" library in Elasticsearch: store historical incidents as document templates with characteristic patterns (e.g., "HSS Sync Failure Signature" = spike in PROCEDURE_CFCQ:"17 UE not authorized" + specific MME host + no transport issues + recent HSS software deployment)
Build real-time pattern matching: use Elasticsearch percolate queries to compare incoming event patterns against signature library, generating suggested root causes automatically
Configure automated runbook integration: when incident signature match occurs, display relevant troubleshooting steps from knowledge base (stored as Elastic Docs) directly in alert notification
Implement feedback loop: NOC engineers can mark suggested root causes as correct/incorrect, feeding supervised learning to improve future recommendations
Create incident post-mortem automation: generate structured RCA documents by querying Elasticsearch for all events within incident time window, organized by correlation strength

Key Metrics:

event.correlation_id linking related events across different network domains
incident.suggested_root_cause and incident.confidence_score from pattern matching
incident.similar_historical_count showing how many past incidents match current pattern
incident.resolution_time_historical_avg from similar past incidents to set expectations
correlation.strength indicating how closely events are temporally and causally related
Time-to-diagnosis improvement: tracking how quickly root cause is identified with vs without automated correlation

Output/Benefits:

Automated diagnosis: "Alert triggered for MME-SF-01 showing 340 authentication failures - Elastic correlation engine automatically identifies: matching signature for 'HSS Sync Failure' (89% confidence based on 23 historical similar incidents), suggests running HSS sync validation script (runbook link provided), historical MTTR for this issue: 18 minutes"
Knowledge capture: New NOC engineers benefit from institutional knowledge embedded in incident signatures, reducing dependency on senior staff for routine troubleshooting
Faster resolution: MTTR improvement from 45 minutes (manual correlation) to 15 minutes (automated suggestion + runbook), validated over 6-month measurement period
Proactive prevention: "Pattern matching identifies early indicators of recurring issue (PROCEDURE_CFCQ spikes) 2 days before full incident manifestation, enabling preventive action"


Data Model & Index Strategy
Recommended Index Patterns
network-logs-{YYYY.MM.dd}: Daily indices for high-volume network signaling logs (S1-MME, X2, GTP-C)

Retention: 90 days hot, 180 days warm, 18 months cold (ILM policy)
Rollover: Daily at midnight UTC
Shards: 6 primary shards (designed for 3-node hot tier), 1 replica

network-metrics-{YYYY.MM}: Monthly indices for time-series metrics from RAN/Core (PM counters)

Retention: 7 days hot, 30 days warm, 12 months cold, then downsampled to hourly aggregates
Rollover: Monthly
Shards: 3 primary shards, 1 replica

network-incidents: Single index for incident tracking with lifecycle management

No time-based rollover, but delete after 24 months
Includes nested objects for correlated events and resolution history

subscriber-sessions-{YYYY.MM.dd}: Subscriber session lifecycle tracking

High-cardinality IMSI field, daily indices
Strict access controls (PII contained), 30-day retention then anonymize/delete

Critical Field Mappings
postDate: date (format: strict_date_optional_time)
  - Primary timestamp for all time-series analysis

host: keyword
  - Network element hostname (MME, eNodeB, router)
  - Used for aggregation by infrastructure component

provider: keyword  
  - Network operator identifier (TMO/TMUS for roaming scenarios)

network.cell.id: keyword
  - ECGI/NCGI formatted cell identifier
  - Pattern: MCC-MNC-eNodeB_ID-Cell_ID (e.g., 310260-1-9560842)
  - Critical for cell-level performance analysis

network.imsi: keyword
  - International Mobile Subscriber Identity
  - Cardinality analysis for subscriber impact assessment
  - PII: restricted access, consider hashing at ingestion

network.procedure.id: keyword
  - Procedure type identifier (e.g., "16 UE Initiated Service Request")
  - Maps to 3GPP standards

network.procedure.result_code: keyword
  - Numeric result code from procedure completion
  
network.procedure.result_message: keyword
  - Human-readable failure cause (e.g., "22 Congestion", "17 UE not authorized")
  - Critical for root cause categorization

event.outcome: keyword
  - ECS standard field: "success" | "failure" | "unknown"
  - Derived from procedure result codes

network.radio.rsrp_dbm: scaled_float (scaling_factor: 10)
  - Reference Signal Received Power in dBm
  - Range typically: -44 to -140 dBm

network.cell.prb_utilization_dl_pct: float
  - Physical Resource Block utilization percentage, downlink
  - Range: 0-100

ml.anomaly_score: float
  - Elasticsearch ML anomaly score
  - Range: 0-100, higher indicates more anomalous

correlation.id: keyword
  - Links related events across indices for incident correlation

Implementation Timeline
Phase 1: Foundation & Quick Wins (Weeks 1-4)
Activities:

Deploy Filebeat on 5 representative MME nodes (one per region) for S1-MME log collection
Configure Logstash parsing pipeline for core network signaling logs
Create baseline dashboards: procedure success rates, top failure causes, MME health
Implement first anomaly detection use case: cell site IMSI/Cell ID spike detection with alerting
Train initial NOC team cohort (20 engineers) on Kibana navigation and basic searches

Deliverables:

Proof-of-concept dashboard showing real-time view of 5 MME pools
Automated alerting for critical cell site degradation
Documentation: Filebeat deployment runbook, dashboard user guide

Phase 2: RAN Integration & ML Enablement (Weeks 5-10)
Activities:

Scale Filebeat deployment to all MME/SGW/PGW nodes (80+ network elements)
Integrate RAN PM data: configure Filebeat collection from Ericsson ENM and Nokia NetAct
Deploy Metricbeat on cell site routers for transport metrics
Configure 10 Elasticsearch ML anomaly detection jobs across key KPI categories
Expand dashboards: add geographic heatmaps, cell-level drill-downs, subscriber session views
Implement ServiceNow bi-directional integration for incident correlation

Deliverables:

Nationwide network visibility covering core and RAN domains
ML-based anomaly detection operational for top failure modes
Integrated incident management workflow

Phase 3: Advanced Analytics & Optimization (Weeks 11-16)
Activities:

Implement subscriber-centric tracking with privacy controls
Deploy event correlation engine with incident signature library
Create capacity planning dashboards with forecasting
Build performance benchmarking scorecards for cross-regional comparison
Configure transform jobs for long-term trending and reporting
Develop Canvas command center dashboards for executive visibility

Deliverables:

End-to-end subscriber experience monitoring
Automated root cause suggestion system
Executive reporting package

Phase 4: Scale & Optimization (Weeks 17-24)
Activities:

Complete rollout to all 15 regional NOC teams
Implement ILM policies for data tier optimization (reduce storage costs)
Fine-tune ML models based on 90 days of operational feedback
Conduct NOC workflow optimization: update SOP documents, integrate Elastic into escalation procedures
Deploy mobile/tablet-optimized dashboards for field technicians
Establish KPI governance: formalize metric definitions, SLA thresholds, and review cadence

Deliverables:

Fully operational observability platform across all regions
Documented ROI metrics: MTTR reduction, proactive incident prevention rate, storage cost optimization
Certified NOC teams with operational proficiency


Expected Outcomes & Business Value
Operational Efficiency Gains

Mean Time to Detect (MTTD): Reduced from 30-45 minutes (complaint-driven) to 3-5 minutes (ML anomaly-driven)
Mean Time to Repair (MTTR): Reduced from 2-4 hours to 45-90 minutes through automated correlation and guided troubleshooting
False Positive Rate: 60% reduction in actionable alerts vs noise from legacy threshold-based monitoring
NOC Analyst Productivity: 40% reduction in time spent on manual log correlation, reallocated to strategic optimization work

Subscriber Experience & Revenue Impact

Proactive Issue Prevention: 35% of potential subscriber-impacting incidents caught and resolved before complaint volume escalates
Customer Churn Risk Reduction: Estimated 2.1% reduction in churn attributable to improved network reliability detection
Enterprise SLA Compliance: 99.5% SLA attainment for business customers (up from 97.2%) through proactive monitoring
Complaint Resolution Time: 40% faster resolution when customer care agents have immediate access to network validation data

Financial Justification

Network Downtime Cost Avoidance: $8.2M annual savings from faster incident resolution and proactive prevention
Infrastructure Optimization: $4.5M avoided in unnecessary capacity expansion through data-driven traffic management and optimization
Operational Expense Reduction: $2.1M annual savings from NOC efficiency gains (fewer analyst-hours per incident)
Observability Platform ROI: 340% over 24 months (total investment: $1.8M in licensing, implementation, training)

pain_points: Lack of standardized KPIs and metrics framework across NOC teams preventing enterprise-wide benchmarking
Fragmented subscriber experience visibility across network domains requiring manual correlation across vendor-specific Element Management Systems
Extended MTTR (2-4 hours) due to manual root cause analysis workflows across multiple systems
Reactive troubleshooting driven by subscriber complaints rather than proactive detection, with 15-30 minute MTTD delays
Insufficient real-time analytics for high-cardinality network data with 15-60 minute batch processing delays
Inability to correlate network events with subscriber behavior patterns across disparate systems
use_cases: Automated Network Event Correlation & Root Cause Inference - Reduce time on manual log correlation by automatically linking related events and suggesting probable causes
Subscriber-Centric Experience Monitoring & Troubleshooting - Provide complete visibility into individual subscriber network experience for rapid complaint resolution
End-to-End Network Performance Benchmarking & Optimization - Enable objective performance comparison across cell sites, vendors, regions, and technologies
Multi-Dimensional Network Incident Root Cause Analysis - Enable rapid root cause identification by correlating cell site performance, core network events, transport metrics in unified view
Network Operations Command Center Unified Dashboard - Provide executive and NOC leadership with real-time comprehensive situational awareness
Intelligent Anomaly Detection for Emerging Network Patterns - Leverage ML to detect subtle performance degradation trends and capacity saturation patterns
Proactive Network Capacity Planning & Congestion Management - Use historical trending and predictive analytics to identify cells approaching capacity limits

Real-Time Cell Site Performance Degradation Detection - Proactively identify cell sites experiencing performance degradation before subscriber impact, reducing MTTD from 30+ minutes to under 5 minutes
